stem: A 50-foot cable runs from the top of a utility pole straight down to an anchor point on the ground, making a 40° angle with the ground. Which of the following expressions gives the height of the utility pole, in feet?
explanation: The cable is the hypotenuse (50 feet) and the pole height is the side opposite the 40° angle at the ground. Since sin = opposite/hypotenuse, the height = hypotenuse × sin 40° = 50 sin 40°.

distractorRationale.A: Used cos instead of sin, which would give the horizontal distance from the anchor to the base of the pole rather than the height: 50 cos 40°.
distractorRationale.B: Correct: sin 40° = opposite/hypotenuse, so height = 50 sin 40°.
distractorRationale.C: Used tan instead of sin, which would be correct only if the 50-foot measurement were the horizontal distance to the anchor rather than the cable length: 50 tan 40°.
distractorRationale.D: Divided by sin instead of multiplying, which would find the hypotenuse given the opposite side rather than finding the opposite side given the hypotenuse: 50/sin 40°.
